body.title: Hotfix: reset T-consequence-skill-telemetry gate and result
body.result: reset T-consequence-skill-telemetry: status=sprint, gate=2026-06-19, result cleared — was incorrectly closed by Igor after gate-parsing bug let it through early
body.description:
Igor picked up T-consequence-skill-telemetry overnight despite its gate of '2026-06-19' because _gate_clear found 'T-skill-telemetry-rollup' in the gate text and returned True (that ticket is closed). Igor then wrote a wrong result claiming skill_telemetry.py was never committed — it was committed at 4f592ad + 49d725a. Repair: (1) reset status from closed back to sprint, (2) fix gate to bare '2026-06-19' with no ticket reference, (3) clear the wrong result field.

**Affected files:** ~/.TheIgors/cc_channel/queue.json (queue state repair via cc_queue.py setstatus + gate commands)
**Design rules:** none apply — queue state repair only
**Scope boundary:** T-consequence-skill-telemetry record only; no code changes
**Test plan:** no tests — queue state repair; verify via cc_queue.py show T-consequence-skill-telemetry showing status=sprint and gate=2026-06-19
**Validation:** cc_queue.py show returns status=sprint, gate='2026-06-19', result=null
**Cleanup:** n/a
**Observable via:** cc_queue.py show T-consequence-skill-telemetry returns correct state
